info.title: svg-sprite
info.description: A dotnet tool for building or extracting of an SVG sprite, i.e. a .svg file with child <symbol> tags.

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: Command.Parse, OK: Command.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: ParseResult.Invoke, OK: ParseErrorAction.Invoke, OK: HelpAction.Invoke, OK: ParseResult.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

commands.name: build
commands.description: Build an SVG sprite file from input SVG files.

options.name: --output-file
options.description: The output SVG sprite file which will contain added <symbol> tags.

options.name: --overwrite-existing
options.description: Overwrite existing files. [default: False]

options.name: --html-preview
options.description: Create an HTML page next to the output SVG sprite file, for previewing the symbols inside the SVG sprite. [default: False]

options.name: --minify
options.description: Minify the SVG output, i.e. disable formatting output, not write individual elements on new lines and indent. [default: False]

options.name: --attributes-to-discard
options.description:
The attributes to discard when converting <svg> tag to <symbol> tag or vice versa.
Wildcards can be used: * - Zero or more characters, ? - Exactly one character.
Attributes that should be usually preserved on <symbol> tag:
- viewBox — must be preserved on <symbol> to define coordinate system
- id — required for referencing the symbol later
- fill, stroke, opacity, transform — if used for styling
- class, style — if CSS is applied
- width and height — optional; usually omitted in sprites

options.name: --elements-to-discard
options.description:
The elements to discard when converting <svg> tag to <symbol> tag or vice versa.
Wildcards can be used: * - Zero or more characters, ? - Exactly one character.
Elements that should be usually preserved inside <symbol> tag:
- <path> — most common for icons
- <circle>, <rect>, <line>, <polygon>, <polyline> — basic shapes
- <g> — groups of elements (preserve if used for structure or styling)
- <text> — if your icon includes text
- <use> — if referencing other symbols
- <defs> — only if it contains reusable elements like gradients or filters used inside the symbol

options.name: --id-prefix
options.description: The prefix to add to the id attribute when converting <svg> tag to <symbol> tag or vice versa.

options.name: --id-for-missing
options.description:
The value to use when id attribute is missing or is not specified when converting <svg> tag to <symbol> tag or vice versa.
When there are duplicates, this value will be incremented like symbol-2, symbol-3 etc. [default: symbol]

options.name: --id-replacement-char
options.description:
The replacement character when sanitizing the id attribute when converting <svg> tag to <symbol> tag or vice versa.
Allowed values are '-', '_', '.'. Setting any other value will disable sanitizing [default: -]

options.name: --id-lower-cased
options.description: The value indicating whether to convert the id attribute to lowercase when converting <svg> tag to <symbol> tag or vice versa. [default: True]

options.name: --view-box-override
options.description: The override value to use for all viewBox attributes, e.g. "0 0 24 24" when converting <svg> tag to <symbol> tag or vice versa.

options.name: --comments-preserved
options.description: The value indicating whether to preserve comments when converting <svg> tag to <symbol> tag or vice versa. [default: False]

arguments.name: input-files
arguments.description:
The input SVG files to build an SVG sprite file, i.e. to add as <symbol> tags to the output SVG sprite file. Duplicate files (same name and size and date modified) will be ignored.
Patterns in paths are supported:
- ? matches a single character
- * matches zero or more characters
- ** matches zero or more recursive directories, e.g. a\**\x matches a\x, a\b\x, a\b\c\x, etc.
- [...] matches a set of characters, syntax is the same as character groups in Regex.
- {group1,group2,...} matches any of the pattern groups. Groups can contain groups and patterns, e.g. {a\b,{c,d}*}.

commands.name: extract
commands.description: Extract symbols from an SVG sprite file to individual SVG files.

options.name: --output-directory
options.description: The output directory to write extracted SVG files.

options.name: --overwrite-existing
options.description: Overwrite existing files. [default: False]

options.name: --minify
options.description: Minify the SVG output, i.e. disable formatting output, not write individual elements on new lines and indent. [default: False]

options.name: --attributes-to-discard
options.description:
The attributes to discard when converting <svg> tag to <symbol> tag or vice versa.
Wildcards can be used: * - Zero or more characters, ? - Exactly one character.
Attributes that should be usually preserved on <symbol> tag:
- viewBox — must be preserved on <symbol> to define coordinate system
- id — required for referencing the symbol later
- fill, stroke, opacity, transform — if used for styling
- class, style — if CSS is applied
- width and height — optional; usually omitted in sprites

- <text> — if your icon includes text
- <use> — if referencing other symbols

When there are duplicates, this value will be incremented like symbol-2, symbol-3 etc. [default: symbol]

options.description:

Allowed values are '-', '_', '.'. Setting any other value will disable sanitizing [default: -]

options.name: --id-lower-cased
options.description: The value indicating whether to convert the id attribute to lowercase when converting <svg> tag to <symbol> tag or vice versa. [default: True]

options.name: --view-box-override
options.description: The override value to use for all viewBox attributes, e.g. "0 0 24 24" when converting <svg> tag to <symbol> tag or vice versa.

options.name: --comments-preserved
options.description: The value indicating whether to preserve comments when converting <svg> tag to <symbol> tag or vice versa. [default: False]

arguments.name: input-file
arguments.description: The input SVG sprite file to extract symbols from, i.e. to extract <symbol> tags to individual SVG files.
